title: anthriscus sylvestris var. nemorosa
intro: Anthriscus sylvestris var. nemorosa, also known as wild chervil or woodland chervil, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of wild chervil include its potential to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and improve overall digestive health. Wild chervil can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in cooking as a culinary herb.
